name: Dispute an HOA Fine, Denial, or Common-Area Issue
description: Call the HOA management company or board liaison to dispute a fine, appeal an architectural-review denial, or escalate a common-area maintenance issue — establishing the formal record and timeline required by most HOA CC&Rs (covenants, conditions, and restrictions).
disclaimer: Not legal advice. Habitability + early-termination skills are scripted to PRESERVE legal posture (request things in writing, refuse verbal-only assurances) — but the operator must consult a tenant attorney for actual rights determination.

context.when_to_use: User has received: (a) an HOA fine they believe is improper or excessive, (b) a denial from architectural review on a planned change (paint, fence, solar, landscaping), or (c) an unresolved common-area issue (broken gate, pool maintenance, drainage). Most HOAs have a formal dispute or appeal process with strict deadlines — this call opens that process.
preconditions: User has a copy of the CC&Rs and the bylaws (or knows where to get them).
User has the fine notice, denial letter, or written complaint with date.
User knows the appeal / dispute deadline stated in the notice (commonly 14–30 days).
User has photos / documentation of the alleged violation or the common-area issue.

principles: Read the CC&Rs BEFORE the call — knowing the actual rule cited beats any opinion.
Appeal deadlines are strict. Missing the appeal window in many HOAs forfeits the right to challenge at all.
Board members are volunteers; management-company staff are paid. Both deserve courtesy — but the management-company employee is usually the one to schedule the appeal.
Always request the formal appeal in writing and ask for the rule, section, and citation that the fine or denial is based on. Vague citations don't survive an appeal.
Document selective enforcement. If neighbors are not being fined for the same thing, that's a real defense — but it requires photos and dates, not anecdotes.
Keep the tone professional. HOA disputes are repeated games — you and the board see each other at the mailbox for years.

phrases.opener_fine: Hi, I'm calling about a violation notice I received dated [date] for [property]. I'd like to formally dispute it and understand the appeal process.
phrases.opener_denial: Hi, I'm calling about the architectural-review denial I received on [date] for my [project]. I'd like to appeal — what's the process and the deadline?
phrases.opener_common_area: Hi, I'm calling about a common-area issue at [location] — [one-sentence description]. It's been [unaddressed since X / a recurring problem]. How do I get this on the board agenda?
phrases.ask_for_citation: Can you tell me exactly which section of the CC&Rs or rules this notice cites? I want to look at the specific language.
phrases.ask_for_appeal_process: Walk me through the appeal: do I submit in writing, do I get a hearing, who decides, and what's the deadline?
phrases.raise_selective_enforcement: I've noticed [specific examples — addresses, photos, dates] where the same condition wasn't cited. Is the rule being enforced consistently? I'd like that addressed in my appeal.
phrases.ask_about_due_process: Was there a notice-and-cure period before the fine was levied? Most HOAs require one — I'd like to confirm the procedure was followed.
phrases.graceful_close_appeal_scheduled: Thank you. To confirm: appeal filed in writing by [date], hearing on [date], decision within [X] days. Anything else I should submit before then?
phrases.graceful_close_referral: Understood. Could you also forward this to the board president? I want to make sure they're aware of my appeal directly.

tactics.name: Open with the specific notice and the appeal intent
tactics.script: Use the matching opener. Be specific — date, property, action. Establishes a real dispute, not a vague complaint.

tactics.name: Demand the specific citation
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_citation`. Many fines cite vague 'community standards.' Force them to the section number — that's what an appeal panel reviews.

tactics.name: Lock the appeal process and deadlines
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_appeal_process`. Get the form of appeal, who decides, and the deadlines. Missing the deadline ends the dispute.

tactics.name: Raise selective enforcement IF documented
tactics.when: Other properties have the same condition uncited.
tactics.script: Use `raise_selective_enforcement`. Bring it with documentation (addresses, photos, dates). Without documentation it weakens your case.

tactics.name: Probe due-process compliance
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_due_process`. Many HOAs MUST give a notice-and-cure period; skipping it can void the fine entirely.

tactics.name: Loop in the board president
tactics.script: Use `graceful_close_referral`. Board members can override management staff. Keep tone neutral — you're asking for awareness, not complaining about the staffer.

boundaries: Do NOT skip paying the underlying assessment or dues — pay them under protest if necessary. Non-payment compounds the problem and can result in liens.
Do NOT make claims about board members' personal motives or other owners — stick to the rule and the facts.
Do NOT accept a settlement that requires waiving future challenges to the same rule without operator review.
Do NOT threaten litigation on the first call. Mention the formal appeal as the path.
Do NOT discuss the dispute on social media or community forums — those quotes will surface in the appeal hearing.
success_signals: Appeal form, deadline, and hearing date confirmed.
Management agrees to forward to the board.
Management acknowledges a procedural gap (no notice-and-cure, ambiguous rule).
failure_signals: Management refuses to identify the cited section.
Appeal process is described as 'just pay it.'
Hearing is scheduled past the cure deadline (fine compounds).
Board liaison won't take the call or respond to written appeal.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm appeal deadline, hearing date, what must be submitted, and who will decide. Operator emails a recap within 24 hours.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Submit the written appeal anyway by certified mail to preserve the record. If the dispute involves a significant fine, lien threat, or pattern of selective enforcement, consult an HOA attorney before the hearing.
follow_ups: Pull the CC&Rs section cited and read it carefully before drafting the written appeal.
Collect documentation: photos of the alleged violation, photos of identical uncited conditions on neighboring properties, prior correspondence.
Send the written appeal by both email AND certified mail before the deadline.
Calendar the hearing date and prepare a 2-minute statement.
If the dispute escalates to a lien threat, consult an HOA attorney immediately.

required_user_info: Notice date, property address, CC&R citation (or claim of citation)
Stated fine amount or denial reason
Appeal deadline as stated in the notice
Photos and documentation supporting the dispute
Names of management-company contact and board liaison
